resources.string: Tutorials
Klick\'r automation is based on what is displayed on your screen, which is pretty different from your time-based regular autoclicker.\n\nLearn here how to use it.
Scenario Basics
Learn how to use each Scenario element

A Scenario is the root element of your automation script. It contains a list of ScreenEvents and a list of TriggerEvents.
An Event is a list of ScreenConditions/TriggerConditions to fulfill in order to execute a list of Actions.

 Learn here how to use each element of a Scenario.
Screen Conditions
Verify what\'s displayed on your screen

A Screen Condition is the "when" part of your Screen Event: When an Event's Screen Conditions are fulfilled, then this Event's Actions are executed.
For a Screen Condition to be fulfilled, the content of your device screen must match a given color, image, text or number. 

 Learn here how to use them with simple interactive use cases.
Color Conditions
Verify if a specific color is displayed

A Color Condition checks the color of a specific pixel on your screen and compares it to a target color. It is the lightest condition type, with no image processing involved.\n\n Learn here how to use it to react to color changes on your screen.
Image Conditions
Verify if a specific image is displayed

An Image Condition captures a screenshot of a part of your screen and looks for it later, either at an exact position, anywhere on the screen, or within a defined area. A threshold controls how strict the match must be.\n\n Learn here how to use it to react to images appearing on your screen.
Number Conditions
Verify if a specific number is displayed

A Number Condition reads a number displayed in a defined area on your screen and compares it to a fixed value or a Counter using operators such as equal, greater than, or less than.\n\n Learn here how to use it to react to numeric values displayed on your screen.
Text Conditions
Verify if specific text is displayed
A Text Condition reads text displayed in a defined area on your screen and checks whether it matches a given value.\n\n Learn here how to use it to react to text appearing on your screen.
Counters
Track and use numeric values in your Scenario.

A Counter is a named numeric variable scoped to your Scenario. It starts at a default value and can be read by Conditions or Actions, and written by the Change Counter Action.\n\n Learn here how to use Counters to build smarter automation scripts.
Combine Conditions
Trigger events using multiple conditions together.

Each Event has a condition operator:AND requires all conditions to be fulfilled, while OR requires at least one. Mixing condition types and operators lets you handle complex real-world scenarios.\n\n Learn here how to combine conditions effectively.
Event State
Enable or disable Events dynamically.

Events can be enabled or disabled at runtime using the ToggleEvent Action. This lets you build multistep automation flows where earlier steps gate later ones, or where certain events are only active under specific conditions.\n\n Learn here how to control Event state to build advanced Scenarios.
Trigger Conditions
Verify the state of the Scenario

A Trigger Condition is the \"when\" part of a Trigger Event: it fires based on non-visual signals such as a timer expiring, a Counter reaching a value, or an Android broadcast being received.\n\n Learn here how to use them to build time-based or state-driven automation flows.
Actions
Execute something once your conditions are fulfilled

An Action is executed when an Event\'s Conditions are fulfilled. Actions range from gestures (Click, Swipe) to flow control (ToggleEvent, ChangeCounter, Pause) and system interactions (Intent, Notification, SystemAction, SetText).\n\n Learn here how to use each Action type to build your automation scripts.
